title: 10 best herbal essential oils for hangover
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for hangover are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of hangover, such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. These oils are extracted from various herbs and plants, offering a safe and effective solution for those who indulge in excessive drinking. The best herbal essential oils for hangover include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Cinchona officinalis, Valeriana officinalis, Panax ginseng, Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Matricaria chamomilla, and Melissa officinalis. If left untreated, hangover can severely impact daily life, causing missed work, social isolation, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with hangover because its invigorating and refreshing properties can help alleviate the symptoms of a hangover, such as nausea and headaches. The menthol in peppermint oil can also help to ease stomach cramps and discomfort caused by excessive drinking. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects can soothe an irritated nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and fatigue that often accompany a hangover. Overall, inhaling or applying peppermint essential oils can provide a natural remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Peppermint essential oil can help alleviate nausea, stomach cramps, and diarrhea caused by excessive drinking due to its carminative properties, which ease digestive discomfort.

Antioxidant: Peppermint essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to cells during hangover, reducing inflammation and promoting recovery.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint essential oil contains compounds like menthol, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling in the brain, liver, and other organs caused by excessive alcohol consumption, and alleviating headache and fatigue symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in menthol, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
Leaves: The leaves contain high amounts of menthone, a natural analgesic that can help relieve headaches and migraines caused by excessive drinking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in early morning when dew still present on leaves for optimal oil content.
Rinse leaves gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry with clean cloth.
Steam distill 200g of dried peppermint leaves with 400ml of distilled water for 45 minutes at 100°c.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and contaminants.
Store the final product in dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with hangover because it contains natural compounds that work to alleviate nausea, headaches, and stomach upset. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger help to reduce swelling in the brain and body, which can occur after a night of heavy drinking. Additionally, the digestive benefits of ginger oil can help to soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea. As a result, incorporating herbal ginger essential oils into your hangover relief routine may provide fast and effective relief from symptoms.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Herbal ginger essential oil helps to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea, which is common during a hangover due to its ability to relax the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
Pain reliever: The analgesic properties of herbal ginger essential oil help to alleviate headaches, soreness, and fatigue associated with hangovers by reducing inflammation and pain in the body.

Anti-inflammatory: Herbal ginger essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can lead to a range of symptoms including muscle soreness, joint pain, and stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant are used to make essential oils that help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the ginger plant, contain compounds that aid in digestion and reduce inflammation, making them effective for hangover relief.
Barks: The barks of the ginger plant are used to make essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms of a hangover.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh ginger rhizomes and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the ginger in a food dehydrator at 150°f for 6 hours or until completely dry and brittle.
Grind 1 cup of dried ginger into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Steam distill the powdered ginger in a double boiler with 1 cup of water for 30 minutes to extract essential oils.
Filter the resulting oil mixture through a cheesecloth or paper filter and store it in a dark glass bottle for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with hangover because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. The oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects can also reduce pain and discomfort, while its sedative properties can promote relaxation and help individuals fall asleep faster, allowing for a quicker recovery from a night out. Additionally, lavender's natural antibacterial and antifungal properties may even help combat the bacteria and yeast that contribute to hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Lavender essential oil's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing inflammation and damage to cells.

Calming and Sedative: The calming and sedative effects of lavender essential oil can help alleviate nausea, headaches, and anxiety associated with hangovers, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms.
Digestive Soothing: Lavender essential oil's soothing properties may help ease digestive issues such as stomach cramps, bloating, and nausea, which are common hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English Lavender contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help to calm and soothe the digestive system, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.

Buds: The buds of English Lavender are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate headaches caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny day in mid-summer.
Dry the harvested flowers for at least 3 days using a clean and well-ventilated area.
Use a solvent like hexane or ethanol to extract the essential oil from dried flowers in a ratio of 1:5.
Distill the mixture using steam distillation equipment for approximately 6 hours to produce high-quality essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with hangover because it contains eucalyptol, which acts as a natural pain reliever and decongestant. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate headaches and soothe an upset stomach, while its expectorant qualities can aid in clearing congestion and promoting healthy digestion. Additionally, the oil's refreshing and invigorating aroma can help to revive and revitalize the senses, providing a welcome respite from the discomforts of a hangover.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been shown to reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate headaches, nausea, and fatigue associated with hangovers caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil help neutralize free radicals generated by alcohol, which can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage, reducing the severity of hangover symptoms.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate anxiety, stress, and irritability that often accompany hangovers, making it easier to recover from a night out.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, which can be exacerbated by excessive alcohol consumption and may help alleviate symptoms associated with hangovers.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and discomfort often experienced during a hangover.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning after a gentle rainstorm.
Steam distill the freshly harvested leaves for precisely 45 minutes to extract the essential oils.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass vial.
Add 5 drops of pure lavender essential oil and mix well with the eucalyptus globulus essential oil.
Store the blended essential oil in an amber-colored glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper essential oils helps with hangover because it contains cineole, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When used topically or in aromatherapy, black pepper oil can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue associated with hangovers. Additionally, its warming effects can help stimulate digestion and relieve bloating, making it an effective remedy to soothe the body after a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_properties: Piperine: Piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper oil, helps to alleviate hangover symptoms by inhibiting enzymes that break down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter responsible for memory and cognitive function.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Black pepper essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can lead to headaches, nausea, and fatigue associated with hangovers.
Digestive aid: The oil's carminative and antispasmodic properties help to alleviate digestive discomforts such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea, common symptoms of hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create a stimulating and invigorating oil that helps alleviate hangover symptoms.
Barks: With its ability to absorb and neutralize toxins, bark is used to create an oil that helps detoxify the body and reduce hangover discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppercorns from your garden or local market in sufficient quantity for the recipe.
Dry the harvested peppercorns in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 3 days.
Steam distill the dried peppercorns using a steam distillation apparatus and collect essential oil.
Add 10ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil to 5ml of distilled piper nigrum essential oil.
Store the final mixture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with hangover because it stimulates blood flow to the brain, alleviating headaches and mental fogginess. Its invigorating aroma also helps to clear sinuses and ease nausea, making it a natural remedy for post-hangout discomfort. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and promote liver function, which may aid in removing toxins from the body and speeding up recovery time.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Digestive stimulant properties: Rosemary essential oil has natural digestive stimulating properties that can help stimulate appetite, relieve nausea and vomiting, and promote healthy digestion after a night of heavy drinking.

CNS stimulant properties: Rosemary essential oil's CNS (Central Nervous System) stimulant properties can help increase alertness, improve mental clarity, and reduce fatigue, making it easier to tackle the morning after a hangover.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, making them a suitable part for creating essential oils that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe and calm the body after a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from the plant.
Wash and dry the harvested rosmarinus officinalis leaves thoroughly.
Steam distill the washed and dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves using a still.
Separate the distilled water from the extracted essential oil using a separator funnel.
Store the isolated rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles for preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with hangover because of its natural properties that can alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. The oil's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds help to calm the stomach and reduce discomfort, allowing for faster digestion and absorption of nutrients. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties can help to clear mucus from the lungs and sinuses, relieving congestion and coughing associated with hangover. Overall, thyme essential oil provides a natural remedy to help alleviate hangover symptoms and promote recovery.

Antioxidant: Thyme's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals caused by alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used due to their ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, making them effective in alleviating hangover symptoms.
Buds: Thyme buds contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds that help to calm nausea and alleviate headaches associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in early morning at dawn.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, dry place for 7-10 days.
Steam distill dried leaves using a double boiler setup and pure water.
Collect the resulting essential oil after 30 minutes of distillation time.
Filter the extracted oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with hangover because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system and alleviate nausea. The oil's calming effects on the nervous system can also help to reduce anxiety and headaches caused by excessive drinking. Additionally, sage contains antioxidants that can help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and damage caused by hangover.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in sage essential oil, such as camphor and borneol, can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hangover symptoms like headache, fatigue, and nausea.

Digestive benefits: Sage essential oil has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues, including upset stomach, bloating, and diarrhea, which are common hangover complaints, thanks to its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation after a night of heavy drinking.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of sage contain sesquiterpenes that can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting symptoms associated with hangover.
Barks: Sage barks have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including headaches and digestive issues common in hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or local market in the early morning.
Dry the leaves in small batches at room temperature for 7-10 days to remove excess moisture.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried leaves for 4 hours.
Combine 15ml of the extracted essential oil with 30ml of sweet almond oil and 10ml of jojoba oil in a dark glass bottle.
Store the final blend in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before use on hangover symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with hangover because its calming and soothing properties can ease nausea, headaches, and stomach upset commonly associated with overindulgence. The oil's natural anti-inflammatory compounds can also reduce swelling and discomfort in the brain and body, while its relaxing effects can help to calm an irritated digestive system. Additionally, marjoram's analgesic properties can provide relief from throbbing headaches, making it a valuable natural remedy for hangover recovery.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Sweet Marjoram Essential Oil has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea and cramps associated with hangovers.

Stress relief: The oil's calming and sedative properties can help alleviate anxiety, headache, and fatigue caused by excessive drinking, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.

Pain relief: Sweet Marjoram Essential Oil has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce headaches, migraines, and muscle soreness commonly experienced after a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of sweet marjoram are used due to their ability to soothe and calm the stomach, helping to alleviate nausea and discomfort associated with hangovers.
Flowers: Sweet marjoram flowers are used to create essential oils that help to ease headaches and promote relaxation, making them an effective natural remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum majorana leaves measuring two cups in the early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested origanum majorana leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat setting for 12 hours.
Combine one cup of dried origanum majorana leaves with 500ml of carrier oil in a glass jar and store it at room temperature for seven days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids after two hours and transfer it to a dark glass bottle.
Store the final product in a cool dry place for up to six months, ready to use or further dilute with additional carrier oil as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow essential oils helps with hangover because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headaches, fever, and body aches commonly associated with excessive alcohol consumption. Additionally, its antispasmodic effects can soothe stomach cramps and nausea, while its antioxidant properties can help neutralize the oxidative stress caused by drinking. By using yarrow essential oils, individuals can promote faster recovery and reduce hangover symptoms, making it an effective natural remedy for post-party relief.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Yarrow essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage cells and tissues in the body, which may alleviate hangover symptoms such as fatigue, headaches, and nausea.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Yarrow essential oil help reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which may relieve hangover symptoms like joint pain, muscle aches, and stomach upset.

Digestive Stimulant Properties: Yarrow essential oil's digestive stimulant properties can help stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps that are common hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Seeds: Containing sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce hangover pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality achillea millefolium leaves at the peak of their potency and dry them properly to ensure optimal oil yield.
Steam-distill or hydrodistill the dried achillea millefolium leaves using a still or apparatus specifically designed for essential oil extraction.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil, separating it from water and impurities to produce a high-quality product.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids to prevent degradation and contamination.
Label and date the essential oil accurately, ensuring that it is stored properly and used within a reasonable timeframe.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hangover with herbal essential oils is by blending peppermint, ginger, and lavender oils. Apply a few drops of this blend to the pulse points or inhale it through steam inhalation to help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. Additionally, eucalyptus oil can be used to reduce inflammation and menthol oil for its refreshing properties.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal essential oils are fatigue, stress, and anxiety. Bergamot oil, for example, is known to help alleviate symptoms of fatigue by reducing mental fogginess and promoting a sense of calmness. Lavender oil can soothe an anxious mind and promote relaxation, while peppermint oil can help ease digestive discomfort and reduce nausea similar to hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with hangover because it contains properties that alleviate nausea and digestive discomfort often associated with excessive alcohol consumption. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe the stomach and reduce vomiting. Additionally, its warming and circulation-boosting properties can ease headaches and fatigue caused by dehydration. The oil's natural digestive enzymes also aid in breaking down and metabolizing alcohol, promoting a faster recovery from hangovers. This makes ginger essential oil a popular remedy for those seeking relief from hangover symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
Shogaols: Shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce headaches and body aches caused by hangovers.
Bisabolol: Bisabolol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, contributing to hangover relief.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and nausea-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale also contain gingerols and shogaols, making them a suitable alternative for hangover relief.
Buds: Ginger buds, also known as young shoots, contain a higher concentration of essential oils than mature rhizomes, providing a potent remedy for hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale at dawn to ensure highest concentration of essential oils.
Clean and peel the harvested rhizomes thoroughly to remove dirt and any impurities present.
Steam distill the peeled rhizomes for 3 hours using a steam distillation apparatus with a water to herb ratio of 1:3.
Separate the obtained distillate into essential oil and hydrosol using a separator or distillation apparatus.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a filter paper to remove any impurities and sediment present.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with hangover because it is known to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the brain, alleviating headaches and discomfort. The antioxidants present in the oil may also help to neutralize free radicals caused by excessive drinking, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, the oil's ability to enhance cognitive function and reduce nausea may aid in alleviating hangover symptoms, such as dizziness and fatigue, making it a potential natural remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, potentially alleviating hangover symptoms.
Terpenoids (Ginkgolides): These compounds may help reduce inflammation, improve blood flow, and alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Bilobalide: This terpenoid has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from free radicals and reduce hangover symptoms like fatigue and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make Ginkgo biloba essential oil, which is believed to help alleviate hangover symptoms by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to create an oil that may help alleviate hangover symptoms due to its potential to improve cognitive function and reduce oxidative stress.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are used to make an essential oil that might aid in hangover recovery by reducing inflammation and promoting blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Extract the ginkgo biloba leaves by hand or with a mechanical harvester in early spring when they are fresh.
Dry the extracted leaves in a warm and dry place or with a food dehydrator at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Use a high-quality solvent such as ethanol or hexane to macerate the dried leaves in a ratio of 1 part leaves to 3 parts solvent for 2 hours.
Filter the macerated solution through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids and obtain the herbal extract.
Distill the herbal extract using steam distillation or solvent-based distillation to produce the ginkgo biloba essential oil in 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils helps with hangover because of its unique properties that work to alleviate symptoms. The oil, derived from the Cinchona tree, contains quinine, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. It helps to ease headaches and nausea associated with hangovers. Additionally, the oil's ability to balance the body's natural energy levels and promote relaxation can aid in recovery. By incorporating Cinchona officinalis essential oils into a hangover remedy, individuals can experience relief from hangover symptoms and a faster return to normal functioning.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with hangovers.
Quinovic acid glycosides: These compounds have been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high content of quinine, a natural compound believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Barks: The barks are often used due to their high concentration of alkaloids like quinine, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate hangover symptoms.
Roots: The roots are also used due to their content of alkaloids like quinine, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark at the right time when the bark is rich in quinine alkaloids.
Steam distill the harvested bark using a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator or a distillation apparatus.
Filter the essential oil through a activated charcoal filter to remove impurities and improve its quality.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency and shelf life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with hangover because it is known to alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. The oils contain valerenic acid, which has a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the body and mind after a night of excessive drinking. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties can help to ease stomach discomfort and cramps, making it a popular natural remedy for those suffering from hangover symptoms. It promotes relaxation and reduces inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid has sedative properties, which can help alleviate the anxiety, headaches, and insomnia associated with hangovers.
Valerenal: Valerenal has anti-inflammatory and sedative effects, which can help reduce the pain, inflammation, and discomfort caused by hangovers.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate headaches, muscle pain, and nausea commonly experienced during hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have sedative and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Valeriana officinalis contain a significant amount of valerenic acid, making them a popular choice for essential oil production to aid in hangover relief.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant also contain valerenic acid, which is believed to provide a gentle, soothing effect that can help reduce hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of valeriana officinalis roots in the early morning after the first frost.
Dry the harvested roots at 60c for 3 hours to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steam distill the dried roots for 2 hours using a distillation apparatus with a 10:1 water ratio.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separating funnel and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities and store at 15c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng essential oils helps with hangover because it's known to promote liver function and detoxification, aiding in the removal of toxins from the body. Its adaptogenic properties also help regulate the body's natural response to stress, which can contribute to hangover symptoms. Additionally, Panax ginseng has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headaches and other discomforts associated with hangovers. Its natural invigorating effects can also help alleviate fatigue and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These ginsenosides help reduce hangover symptoms by inhibiting the breakdown of blood-brain barrier and reducing inflammation in the brain.
Terpenoids: Specifically, terpenoids like ginsenoside Rg1 help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Saponins: Saponins in Panax ginseng help mitigate hangover symptoms by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which reduce cell damage and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used for ginseng essential oil production due to their high concentration of active compounds, which help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Leaves: Ginseng leaf essential oil is also used for its adaptogenic properties, which can help reduce hangover-related stress and promote recovery.
Stems: While less commonly used, ginseng stem essential oil may be used in some hangover remedies due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe hangover-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 panax ginseng roots with a diameter of 1-2 cm, and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried roots at a temperature of 100-150 c and a pressure of 0.5-1 bar for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the aqueous phase using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove any impurities and improve its quality.
Store the herbal panax ginseng essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature of 10-20 c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils helps with hangover because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Turmeric, the active compound in Curcuma longa, reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are often exacerbated by excessive alcohol consumption. It also has natural pain-relieving and antispasmodic effects, which can alleviate headaches and stomach discomfort commonly experienced after a night of heavy drinking. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Curcuma longa essential oils can provide relief and support a smoother recovery.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, contributing to hangover symptoms.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and fatigue.
Tumerone: It has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which could help mitigate the damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption and reduce hangover severity.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used to make essential oils for hangover relief because they contain compounds like curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Roots: They are used for essential oils because they contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation, pain, and nausea associated with hangovers.
Leaves: They are used for essential oils because they contain volatile oils and bioactive compounds that can help alleviate hangover symptoms, such as headaches and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh rhizomes and wash them thoroughly with water to remove dirt and impurities.
Steam distill the rhizomes in a 1:5 ratio with water for 3 hours to obtain the oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separator or a centrifuge to collect 2ml.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities and improve clarity.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with hangover because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The licorice root oil contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties may help to protect the liver and promote its natural detoxification process, which can aid in reducing the severity of hangover symptoms. This natural remedy can provide relief and comfort after a night of excessive drinking.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: Help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Phenolic acids (specifically, glycyrrhizin): Assist in reducing hangover severity by inhibiting the activity of prostaglandins, which contribute to nausea, headaches, and stomach discomfort.

Flavonoids (specifically, licoricidin and licorisoflavan A): Help mitigate hangover symptoms by acting as antioxidants, neutralizing free radicals that cause tissue damage and promoting the repair of damaged cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate hangover symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to alleviate hangover symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils, which are thought to contain compounds that can help to reduce inflammation, nausea, and other hangover-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning, after the dew has evaporated, and dry them thoroughly within 24 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 1 liter of water at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillation apparatus and transfer it to a glass container.
Filter the essential oil using a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil in a cool, dark place, away from heat and light sources, for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps with hangover because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that soothe the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of nausea and stomach discomfort. The oil's anethole compound has been shown to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramping and spasms associated with hangovers. Additionally, its mild sedative effects can help calm the mind and body, promoting a sense of well-being and reducing the severity of hangover symptoms. This makes Foeniculum vulgare essential oil a popular natural remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, helping to reduce hangover symptoms such as nausea and headaches.
Fenchone: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from hangover-related pain and discomfort.
Beta-pinene: Demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate hangover symptoms such as inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Foeniculum vulgare are used for their digestive benefits, which may help alleviate some hangover symptoms.
Leaves: Fennel leaves have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce hangover discomfort.
Flowers: Fennel flowers have been used to aid digestion and relieve nausea, which can be beneficial for hangover relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 1 kg of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Clean the harvested material with a gentle stream of water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the cleaned foeniculum vulgare material at a temperature of 100-105°c for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and sediment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla essential oils helps with hangover because it contains apigenin, an antioxidant that calms the mind and soothes the body. The soothing properties of chamomile oil help reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, alleviating symptoms of a hangover such as headaches and nausea. Additionally, its calming effects can help regulate the body's natural rhythms, promoting a restful night's sleep and facilitating a faster recovery from a night of overindulgence. Its natural properties can also help to reduce stress and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: It helps with hangover by reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain.
α-Bisabolol oxide: It helps with hangover by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help to mitigate hangover-related inflammation and oxidative stress.
Apigenin: It helps with hangover by acting as a sedative and anti-inflammatory, which can help to reduce anxiety and alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe hangover symptoms.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing hangover discomfort.
Seeds: They are a good source of apigenin, which helps alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day.
Dry the harvested flowers in a cool dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steam distill the dried flowers at a temperature of 100°c and a pressure of 2 bar for 3-4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a separatory funnel within 30 minutes of distillation.
Store the obtained essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and a label with its contents and date of production.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with hangover because of its soothing and calming properties. It is known to reduce inflammation and relax the body, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with a hangover. The oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, making it easier to recover from a night of excessive drinking. Additionally, Melissa officinalis oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to ease anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinuscine (Rosmarinic acid): Helps with hangover by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by excessive drinking.
Linalool: Helps with hangover by acting as a natural anxiolytic, reducing anxiety and stress associated with hangover symptoms.
Limonene: Helps with hangover by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of lemon balm's active compounds, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and calming effects.
Flowers: Used for their unique fragrance and ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress caused by hangovers.
Stems: Used in combination with leaves and flowers for their additional medicinal properties, which may aid in soothing digestive issues associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1000g of melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure freshness.
Steam distill the leaves at 100 degrees celsius for 3 hours to release their volatile compounds.
Collect the essential oil in a separate container as it separates from the distilled water.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and particles.
Store the melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat.
